note: Sol recovery consult: fixable-same-angle endpoint-plus-inference reframe
directive: DIRECTIVE: Rebuild angle 0 around “sharp common-coupling identification and regular/nonregular inference for a nonseparable two-sided gain-band functional under discrete-IV generalized Roy,” deleting the association-loss sign-flip/breakdown-frontier headline. Let \(r_{mkij}=\Pr(U\in I_k,Y(0)=y_i,Y(1)=y_j\mid X=x_m)\) and correct the factual map to \([A_mr]_{1\ell h}=\sum_{k:q_k\le p_{m\ell}}\sum_i r_{mkih}\) and \([A_mr]_{0\ell h}=\sum_{k:q_{k-1}\ge p_{m\ell}}\sum_j r_{mkhj}\). Include the required \(K=1,J=2,L=2,p=(0,1)\) hand audit: the \(p=1\) arm reveals \(\sum_i r_{m1ih}=\Pr(Y(1)=y_h\mid X=x_m)\), the \(p=0\) arm reveals \(\sum_j r_{m1hj}=\Pr(Y(0)=y_h\mid X=x_m)\), and drawing \((Y(0),Y(1))\) from that single table, \(U\sim U[0,1]\), \(Z\perp(Y(0),Y(1),U)\mid X\), and \(D=1\{U\le p\}\) supplies the common-law pasting. Specify the analyst’s copula sensitivity as a polyhedral restriction \(\mathcal R(\epsilon)=\{r:H_\epsilon r\le h_\epsilon\}\), define \(\mathcal F_\epsilon(P)=\{r\ge0:\sum_{ij}r_{mkij}=\lambda_k,\ A_mr=b_m,\text{ displayed rank inequalities},\ r\in\mathcal R(\epsilon)\}\), and restrict endpoints to \(\mathcal D_\epsilon=\{P:\alpha_m,\pi_{\ell m}>0,\text{ the stated design conditions hold},\mathcal F_\epsilon(P)\ne\varnothing\}\). Call exact cutpoint coverage a deliberately discrete cutpoint-saturated design subclass, never standard support. For \(c_{ij}=1\{a\le y_j-y_i\le b\}\), define \(\psi_\omega(r)=\sum_{mkij}\alpha_m\omega_kc_{ij}r_{mkij}\) and \((\psi_L,\psi_U)=(\min,\max)_{r\in\mathcal F_\epsilon(P)}\psi_\omega(r)\); this is a linear LP, so do not invoke Charnes–Cooper unless an explicitly chosen target is \(N(r)/D(r)\) with affine \(N,D\) and \(D\ge c>0\). Make the strict advance concrete with the named witness class \(\mathcal W_\kappa\): in the audited binary model, take both potential-outcome marginals Bernoulli \(1/2\), band \([0,0]\), rank scores \((-1,1)\), and \(S(r)=E[\rho(Y(0))\rho(Y(1))]\ge\kappa\in(0,1)\); writing \(t=\Pr(Y(0)=Y(1))\) gives \(S=2t-1\), hence the sharp common-coupling band set is \([(1+\kappa)/2,1]\), strictly inside the marginal/unrestricted Fréchet set \([0,1]\). State this as strict non-representability by separable/product-cost or assembled marginal/pointwise bounds, not as tightening those papers’ different estimands. Correct Gao–Ge–Qian to Zijun Gao, Shu Ge, and Jian Qian, “Bridging Multiple Worlds: Multi-marginal Optimal Transport for Causal Partial-identification Problem,” AISTATS 2025, PMLR 258:721–729; correct Han–Yang to Sukjin Han and Shenshen Yang, “A Computational Approach to Identification of Treatment Effects for Policy Evaluation,” Journal of Econometrics 240(1), 105680 (2024). Compare systematically: Masten–Poirier supplies generic breakdown-frontier logic but is no longer the headline; Tan–Blanchet–Syrgkanis and Marx cover separable/linear generalized-Roy or latent-index functionals; Han–Yang covers sharp weighted-MTE policy bounds, not joint gain events; Gao–Ge–Qian supplies general causal MOT; Fan–Guerre–Zhu studies joint-potential-outcome functionals after marginal recovery; Firpo–Ridder and Frandsen–Lefgren establish pointwise-versus-common-coupling issues; Ober-Reynolds supplies regular OT-dual inference without generated Roy constraints; Fang–Santos supplies the directional resampling framework. Retain four honest solve targets: (1) a proved/routine factual-map-and-pasting lemma including the audit; (2) a main Conjecture, marked NOT YET PROVED, that \(\operatorname{Id}_P^\epsilon(\psi)=[\psi_L,\psi_U]\) with attainment plus the strict \(\mathcal W_\kappa\) result; (3) an inference Conjecture, not an OEQ, deriving the centered augmented dual score \(\phi_s(W)=\partial_\eta L_s(r_s^*,\lambda_s^*;\eta_0)[IF_\eta(W)]\), proving Neyman orthogonality and root-\(n\) asymptotic normality under primitive separated/stable cutpoint incidence, a locally constant active set, unique dual, feasibility slack, and nuisance product rates, and proving a derivative-estimated Fang–Santos directional bootstrap when the optimal dual set is non-singleton; and (4) a non-headline separable-cost reduction proposition. Do not call the estimated incidence/cutpoint map differentiable: either treat it as known design information or impose a primitive positive separation margin and fixed ordering/multiplicity making it locally stable. Finally synchronize `specialization`, `tldr`, `project_justification`, `related_work`, every comparator-table relation, `honest_scope`, and the proposal text to this repaired endpoint-plus-inference headline; if the strict witness or inference regularity statement cannot be retained, declare the angle terminal rather than reverting to a sign-radius frontier.
